back_translation: Some Pharisees and Sadducees came to Jesus to test Him, asking Him to show them a sign from heaven.
But He replied, "When evening approaches, you say, 'Tomorrow the weather will be fair, because the sky is red,'
and in the morning, 'Today there will be a storm, because the sky is red and overcast.' You know how to discern the appearance of the sky, but you cannot discern the signs of the times.
An evil and adulterous generation seeks a sign, but no sign will be given to them except the sign of Jonah." Then He left them and went away.
When the disciples crossed to the other side, they forgot to take bread with them.
Jesus said to them, "Watch out and beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees."
They discussed among themselves, saying, "He says this because we did not bring bread."
Jesus, knowing this, said, "You of little faith, why are you discussing among yourselves that you did not bring bread?
Do you still not understand? Do you not remember the five loaves for the five thousand and how many baskets you collected?
Or the seven loaves for the four thousand and how many baskets you collected?
How is it that you do not understand that I was not speaking about bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees instead."
Then they understood that He was not telling them to beware of the leaven used to make bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
When Jesus came into the region of Caesarea Philippi, He asked His disciples, "Who do people say that the Son of Man is?"
They answered, "Some say John the Baptist; some say Elijah; some say Jeremiah or one of the prophets."
He said to them, "But you — who do you say I am?"
Simon Peter answered, "You are the Christ, the Son of the living God."
Jesus replied, "Simon son of Jonah, you are blessed, because it was not flesh and blood that revealed this to you, but my Father who dwells in heaven.
And I myself tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of the realm of the dead shall not prevail against that church.
I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven, and whatever you bind on the earth shall be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on the earth shall be loosed in heaven."
Then He charged the disciples not to tell anyone that He was the Christ.

From that time on, Jesus began to show His disciples that it was necessary for Him to go to Jerusalem, to suffer many things from the elders, the chief priests, and the scribes, to be put to death, and on the third day to rise again.
Peter took Him aside and began to remonstrate with Him, saying, "Lord, may God forbid such a thing! This shall by no means happen to You."
But He turned and said to Peter, "Get behind me, you Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, because you are not thinking the things of God, but the things of men."
Then Jesus said to His disciples, "If anyone wants to come after me, that one must deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.
For whoever wants to preserve his life will lose his life, but whoever willingly loses his life for my sake will find his life.
For if a person gains the whole world as his own but loses his life, what benefit is there? Or what can a person give in exchange for his life?
For the Son of Man will come in the glory of the Father with His angels, and then He will repay each person according to his deeds.
Truly I say to you, among those standing here some will not taste death until they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom."
